description_long: This is a two-story, rectangular house with a weathered, beige stucco exterior and visible signs of aging and wear. The facade features tall, narrow windows with dark wooden frames, some of which have arched tops on the upper floor, and a single main entry door with a transom window above it. The lower portion of the walls shows exposed brickwork and peeling plaster, indicating age and lack of recent maintenance. The windows are arranged in a mostly symmetrical pattern, with some minor variations between sides, and the upper windows are slightly larger and more ornate. The roof is flat and bordered by a low parapet, typical of older urban residential buildings. The overall appearance suggests a late 19th or early 20th-century European urban architectural style.
description: A weathered, two-story beige stucco house with tall, narrow windows, a flat roof, and a single main entry door. The facade shows signs of age, with peeling plaster and exposed brickwork.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry door, three lower windows, and four upper windows with arched tops and weathered stucco.
description_view_1: Side view with two upper windows and two lower windows, including a white-painted door and visible wall aging.
description_view_2: Nearly identical to the front, with the main entry door, symmetrical window arrangement, and visible wall damage.
description_view_3: Rear or alternate side view with two upper and two lower windows, lacking a door and showing more uniform window placement.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with arched upper windows
attribute_entryway_design: Single main door with a rectangular transom window, no porch or steps, simple trim
